title: Top 50 Common English Mistakes Hindi Speakers Make
teacherNote: यह mistakes EVERY Hindi speaker करता है। इन्हें अच्छे से पढ़ो और याद रखो। अगर तुम इन 50 mistakes से बचना सीख गए, तो tumhari English 70% improve हो जाएगी।

mistakes.rule: He/She/It + verb+s/es
She/He/It → doesn't, not don't
Know = stative verb → Simple Present only
Have → Has with He/She/It
Tell + object (no 'to': tell someone, NOT tell to someone)
'Agree' is a verb, not adjective — no 'am' before it
Information = uncountable — no plural

mistakes.wrong: She is more taller than me.
mistakes.rule: Don't use 'more' with -er comparatives — double comparative error
WH Questions need Do/Does
Age uses 'am/is/are ... old', NOT 'have'
'Very much' is used with verbs/nouns; 'very' with adjectives

mistakes.correct: She explained the topic to me.
mistakes.rule: 'Explain' needs 'to': explain something to someone

mistakes.wrong: My mother is cooking the food since morning.
mistakes.correct: My mother has been cooking since morning.
mistakes.rule: 'Since' needs Present Perfect (Continuous)
'Cousin' already means male/female — no need to add 'sister/brother'
Didn't + base verb (not past form)

mistakes.wrong: He is working in this office from 2020.
mistakes.correct: He has been working in this office since 2020.
mistakes.rule: Continuous action since a point → Present Perfect Continuous + since

mistakes.wrong: I want to tell you a doubt.
mistakes.correct: I want to ask you a question. / I have a doubt.
mistakes.rule: 'Tell a doubt' is wrong — you ask questions or have doubts

mistakes.wrong: She cut the vegetables with a knife.
mistakes.correct: She cut the vegetables with a knife. ✓
mistakes.rule: This is actually correct! 'With' for instrument/tool

mistakes.wrong: Do you know where is the station?
mistakes.correct: Do you know where the station is?
mistakes.rule: Indirect questions: normal word order (no inversion)

mistakes.wrong: Yesterday I have seen that movie.
mistakes.rule: Past time marker (yesterday) → Simple Past (not Present Perfect)

mistakes.wrong: He is more smarter than her.
mistakes.rule: Double comparative error — 'more smarter' wrong

mistakes.wrong: I will go at the market.
mistakes.correct: I will go to the market.
mistakes.rule: Movement direction uses 'to' (not 'at')
'Beautiful looking' is redundant — use 'looks beautiful' or 'very beautiful'
'Reach' doesn't need 'to' — reach = arrive at
'Discuss' doesn't need 'about'
Stative verbs (know/love/have/understand) → Simple Present only
'Understand' needs do/don't for negation — not am/isn't
Imperative question tag = 'will you?' or 'won't you?'

mistakes.wrong: The scenery of this place are beautiful.
mistakes.correct: The scenery of this place is beautiful.
mistakes.rule: 'Scenery' is uncountable → singular verb 'is'
'Very much' before adjectives is wrong — use 'very'
'Both...not' is wrong — use 'neither'

mistakes.wrong: He said me that he was tired.
mistakes.correct: He told me that he was tired.
mistakes.rule: 'Say to someone' OR 'tell someone' (NOT 'say me')
'Very much' goes at the end of sentence
'Real sister' is redundant in most contexts

mistakes.wrong: I have a doubt on this topic.
mistakes.correct: I have a question about this topic.
mistakes.rule: 'Doubt about' not 'doubt on'; better: have a question
'Eldest' is superlative (used with 'the'); 'older' for comparison

mistakes.correct: Are you able to come? / Can you come?
mistakes.rule: 'Can' and 'able to' both mean same — use one
Countable noun needs article
'Have' for possession/state → Simple Present; no continuous
'Resemble' is stative → Simple Present

mistakes.wrong: She is belonging to this club.
mistakes.rule: 'Belong' is stative → Simple Present
It's = It is; Its = possession. Different meaning!

mistakes.wrong: I will revert back to you.
mistakes.rule: 'Revert' already means 'to come back' — 'back' is redundant

mistakes.correct: Please do what is required. / Please take necessary action.
mistakes.rule: 'Do the needful' is an outdated Indian English expression — avoid in professional contexts

mistakes.correct: I am out of town. / I am traveling.
mistakes.rule: 'Out of station' is Indian English — international English uses 'out of town'

mistakes.correct: I am John. / My name is John.
mistakes.rule: 'Myself' used alone as subject is wrong — very common Indian English error
'Genius' is a noun, not adjective — cannot use 'very' with it directly

mistakes.correct: She has a lot of knowledge.
mistakes.rule: 'A lot of' not 'lot of' — article 'a' is required
'Advice' is uncountable — no plural form

mistakes.wrong: The students were discussed the topic.
mistakes.rule: Active voice doesn't need 'were' before main verb
